titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.46

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=732 status=reviewed modified=2023-03-13 15:02:37.17535+00 | translation moa=733 status=reviewed modified=2023-03-13 15:02:37.163873+00

p.cl: Henricus Comes filius Regis scocie omnibus fidelibus hominibus suis salutem .

concessisse et dedisse Vctredo Lond’ . vii . viginti acras terre in hangre de
Toteham . et dimidivm holmum quem Engelramus habuit . et . iiii . trunkos ad ignem suum
de bosco meo de Toteham . et . decem porcos quietos sine pasnagio tenendum de me et
de heredibus meis . illi et heredibus suis finabiliter cum sacka et socca et tol . et team et
omnibus libertatibus que ad terram pertinent . reddendo unoquoque anno . i . marcam argenti ad festum Sancti
Michaelis pro omnibus seruiciis .

t’ Dauidis . patris mei Regis Scocie . et Hugonis de Morevill’ . et
Hereberti Camerarii . Roberti de Brus . et Roberti Foliot et plurium aliorum
Henry the Earl, son of the King of Scotland, to all his sworn men [sends] greeting.

Know me to have granted and to have given to Uhtred of London 140 [7 × 20] acres of land in the Hanger of Tottenham and the half holm that Ingram had, and 4 tree trunks for his fire from my wood of Tottenham, and ten pigs quit of pasture dues for him and his heirs to hold of me and my heirs forever with sake and soke and toll and team and all liberties that pertain to the land, by rendering each year 1 merk of silver at the feast of St Michael in return for all services.
By the witness of David my father, and Hugh de Morville, and of Herbert the chamberlain, and of Robert de Bruce, and of Robert Foliot, and of many others.
